frameworks.name: variational Bayesian inference
frameworks.one_line: tractable approximation to full posterior over latent variables and functions

frameworks.name: Gaussian process classification
frameworks.one_line: GP priors replace coefficient priors for multiclass classification

frameworks.name: data augmentation with Gaussian latent variables
frameworks.one_line: auxiliary latents convert multinomial probit into conditionally Gaussian model

frameworks.name: Markov Chain Monte Carlo and Laplace approximation
frameworks.one_line: exact and alternative approximate inference baselines

core_question: Can a fully variational Bayesian treatment yield accurate, efficient multiclass GP classification without extra approximations to the non-Gaussian likelihood?
key_assumptions: GP priors over class-specific latent functions are a priori independent
multinomial probit link with Gaussian auxiliary latents permits exact conditional analysis
factorized variational posterior is adequate for class coupling
manipulations: inference scheme varied: variational vs MCMC vs Laplace
sparse approximation via informative vector machine selection
key_findings: variational approximation matches MCMC accuracy at lower computational cost
variational method is more accurate than the Laplace approximation
sparse multiclass IVM approximations emerge naturally from the formulation
interpretations: data augmentation plus variational Bayes is a computationally economic alternative to full MCMC for multiclass GP classification
